prep.summary: You must prepare for the upcoming workload balancing session by reviewing the key takeaways from the previous alignment meeting with Praveena—specifically the need to establish a ground-up view of actual team capacity rather than relying strictly on Jira data. The primary objective remains relieving the heavily strained claims team—this involves addressing severe resource bottlenecks in data architecture and Celonis-related initiatives. You will need to leverage the outcomes from this prep session to drive the broader team workshop—ensuring an open dialogue while shielding the team from premature leadership oversight.
tasks: Establish a ground-up capacity baseline for the Enterprise Architecture team instead of relying solely on Jira metrics.
Request the part-time return of Chris to alleviate the high workload burden on data architecture and third-party integrations.
Prioritize the Celonis process mining mandates to resolve resource contention across parallel visualization initiatives.
Deliver a comprehensive summary of the workload workshop outcomes to leadership in lieu of their direct attendance.
prep.title: P&C Architecture Workload Balancing Discussion

prep.summary: You face critical resource bottlenecks across parallel Celonis initiatives—driven by Dan Griffiths's 90-day mandate—and severe data architecture constraints. Security mandates have ruled out transactional SMS—forcing a strategic pivot to secure asynchronous chat to protect the P&C business case. Additionally, you must navigate a potential 50% funding cap on the Claims Evolution program—threatening its $1.5 billion valuation—alongside immature AI capabilities.
tasks: Resolve the resource contention surrounding the Celonis process mining pilot by prioritizing Dan Griffiths's 90-day mandate over lower-priority visualization projects.
Pivot the current SMS proof of concept toward an asynchronous secure chat model to satisfy strict security and NYDFS compliance requirements.
Hold the scheduled workload balancing workshop to assess team capacity and finalize bringing Chris back part-time to relieve the strained data architecture team.
Address the financial and member-experience implications of the potential 50% pacing reduction on the Claims Evolution program.
prep.title: Team Lunch/Ask me Anything (series ends 4/15/27)

events.summary: Microsoft Teams Office Hours: Live Demos and Q&A (Session 1)

prep.summary: Security assessments have blocked transactional two-way SMS—deeming it a non-starter due to regulatory compliance—which forces you to pivot the 71-account proof of concept toward secure asynchronous chat and low-risk logistical coordination. Meanwhile, resource bottlenecks threaten the business process health scorecard—the BPO notebook—as Celonis efforts are split between this pilot, claims visualization, and a priority 90-day mining mandate. You must also finalize the incomplete BPO architecture diagram left outstanding by Emmitt before his holiday.
tasks: Pivot the two-way SMS proof-of-concept scope—focusing strictly on non-secure logistical claims coordination—to comply with security mandates.
Finalize the incomplete BPO notebook architecture diagram and Wiki draft left outstanding before Emmitt's holiday.
Reallocate strained Celonis resources to balance the BPO notebook pilot against the higher-priority 90-day process mining mandate.
Address the business friction caused by the strict NYDFS-driven security rejection of transactional SMS.
prep.title: D&S Transformation Tech Partner Connect
